perturbation.original_text: 1.02 LOCAL OFFICE (AFFILIATE OFFICE). Affiliate shall locate, establish and maintain such offices (the "Local Office"), as it deems necessary for use by all Contractors identified by Affiliate and accepted by Network 1. Network 1 expressly agrees that Affiliate shall not be geographically restricted in its establishment of such offices. Local Office has met the satisfaction of Network 1 in terms of space, quality, appearance, equipment, supplies, office hours, support staff, working conditions, and other factors materially affecting the working conditions of its Contractors. All Local Offices shall bear signs, emblems and other insignia, including a telephone number, indicating that the office is in fact an office of Network 1 subject to the conditions of 4.02. However, all leases, utility listing, telecommunication, furniture and equipment expense, employment contracts for support personnel and other contracts relating to the operation of the Local Office shall be in the name of the Affiliate and shall be the sole responsibility of the Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.02 LOCAL OFFICE (AFFILIATE OFFICE). Affiliate shall locate, establish and maintain such offices (the "Local Office"), as it deems necessary for use by all Contractors identified by Affiliate and accepted by Network 1. Network 1 expressly agrees that Affiliate shall be geographically restricted in its establishment of such offices. Local Office has met the satisfaction of Network 1 in terms of space, quality, appearance, equipment, supplies, office hours, support staff, working conditions, and other factors materially affecting the working conditions of its Contractors. All Local Offices shall bear signs, emblems and other insignia indicating that the office is in fact an office of Network 1 subject to the conditions of 4.02. However, all leases, utility listing, telecommunication, furniture and equipment expense, employment contracts for support personnel and other contracts relating to the operation of the Local Office shall be in the name of the Affiliate and shall be the sole responsibility of the Affiliate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase ", including a telephone number,", the modified text omits a previously specified requirement for Local Offices. This creates an internal contradiction because the agreement still states that Local Offices should bear certain insignia indicating their affiliation with Network 1, but now the specific inclusion of a telephone number is absent. This creates uncertainty, given the phrase 'including', makes it uncertain, if a telephone number is required as part of insignia. The modified 1.02 paragraph is contradicted with 4.02, because 4.02 references to the Local Office insignia, so it creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.02 NETWORK 1 NAME USAGE. Affiliate shall use the Network 1 name in Relationship to all Bankcard marketing activity as required by the rules of VISA USA, International and MasterCard International. Affiliate acknowledges that the use of the Network 1 name is on a non-exclusive basis and further agrees to cease using Network 1 name, including but not limited to logo(s) and insignia(s) at the written request of Network 1. In the event this contract terminates for any reason, Affiliate shall immediately cease using the Network 1 name. The insignia, logo's, Service Marks, trademarks and name of Network 1 are the Source: USIO, INC., SB-2, 4/28/2004 absolute and sole right of Network 1 Financial Corporation, a Virginia Corporation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 1.02 removes the explicit requirement of including a telephone number on the Local Office's insignia, but 4.02 references 'insignia(s)' of Network 1. The removal creates ambiguity about whether a telephone number is still implicitly required as part of the insignia, leading to a contradiction. The original text included 'telephone number', and by removing that, it is now uncertain if it is still needed, which contradicts 4.02

perturbation.original_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate shall not approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, fines, and administration expense.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate shall not approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, and administration expense.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'fines,' from the sentence 'If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, fines, and administration expense', creates ambiguity and potential contradiction. While Affiliate remains responsible for 'any expense', the omission of 'fines' could be interpreted as an explicit exclusion. This creates uncertainty as the expense incurred can potentially be because of fines or other monetary charges, where an specific example was removed. The removed word can cause a internal contradiction in the paragraph, as any expense sounds like anything, but removing fines, sounds like excluding them, by not mentioning it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate shall not approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, and administration expense.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'fines' from the list of expenses the Affiliate is responsible for upon termination for cause, while the contract states Affiliate is responsible for 'any expense', creates a contradiction. The omission can be interpreted as an explicit exclusion of fines, contradicting the broader statement 'any expense'. This creates ambiguity and potential for disputes, as Network 1 could argue that 'any expense' includes fines, while the Affiliate could argue that the specific omission implies otherwise.

perturbation.original_text: 8.05 GOVERNING LAW. All disputes or claims by Payment Data Systems hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in McLean, Virginia, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association. All disputes or claims by NETWORK 1 hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in San Antonio, Texas, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association. 8.06 ARBITRATION. All disputes or claims hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in McLean, Virginia, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.05 GOVERNING LAW. All disputes or claims by Payment Data Systems hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in McLean, Virginia, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association. All disputes or claims by NETWORK 1 hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in San Antonio, Texas, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association. 8.06 ARBITRATION. All disputes or claims hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in San Antonio, Texas, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'McLean, Virginia' to 'San Antonio, Texas' in section 8.06, it now contradicts the governing law specified in 8.05 for Payment Data Systems. Section 8.05 clearly states arbitration for Payment Data Systems should be in McLean, Virginia, yet 8.06 ambiguously states all disputes will be resolved in San Antonio, Texas (making no distinction on which party). This creates a direct in-text contradiction about the location for arbitration for Payment Data Systems.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.05 GOVERNING LAW. All disputes or claims by Payment Data Systems hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in McLean, Virginia, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association. All disputes or claims by NETWORK 1 hereunder shall be resolved by arbitration in San Antonio, Texas, pursuant to the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 8.06 modifies the arbitration location to 'San Antonio, Texas' for 'All disputes or claims hereunder.' This directly contradicts section 8.05, which specifies that disputes for Payment Data Systems should be resolved in 'McLean, Virginia.' The change creates an in-text contradiction as to the arbitration location for Payment Data Systems, fulfilling the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition, where 'essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.'
